title: Housing Search Assistance * Former Foster Children - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Housing & Shelter > Housing Search Assistance * Former Foster Children 1 Matching Service Providers Refine Results - Select - No Filter AIDS/HIV Issues Domestic Violence Issues Former Foster Children Foster Children Hispanic/Latino Community Homeless Families Homeless Youth Homelessness Issues Teen Pregnancy Issues Veteran/Military Personnel Issues Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF OREGON STATE DEPARTMENT OF HUMAN SERVICES OREGON STATE DEPARTMENT OF HUMAN SERVICES CHILD WELFARE ADMINISTRATIVE OFFICE INDEPENDENT LIVING PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF The Independent Living Program (ILP) helps youth who are, or were, in foster care to become self-sufficient adults. It gives an opportunity to learn valuable skills necessary to make a successful transition from state and Tribal custody to living on their own in the community. Services include access to case management and funds for continuing education and assistance with housing. 500 Summer Street NE Suite E67 Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: Current or former foster children ages 16-20 Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (503) 945-6969 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON STATE DEPARTMENT OF HUMAN SERVICES INDEPENDENT LIVING PROGRAM
